Group A.longDescription: Mexico's squad for the 2026 World Cup represents a blend of experience, identity, and renewal. Led by Javier Aguirre, it brings together historical icons with a new generation of players, aiming for a historic performance on home soil. The team maintains its competitive style, intensity, and strong connection with its fans, with the goal of delivering a solid and dominant performance on home soil.
Group A.shortDescription: Mexico's team for the 2026 World Cup combines experience and youth, seeking to represent its footballing identity and stand out as the tournament's host.
Group A.title: Mexico

Group A.longDescription: South Africa's selection for the 2026 World Cup represents speed, energy, and growth, with a team that seeks to compete at the highest level and leave its mark on the tournament.
Group A.shortDescription: South Africa's selection for the 2026 World Cup represents speed, energy, and growth, with a team that seeks to compete at the highest level and leave its mark on the tournament.
Group A.title: South Africa

Group A.longDescription: South Korea arrives at the 2026 FIFA World Cup as one of Asia’s most established football nations, combining experienced leaders with a dynamic generation of talent. Led by coach Hong Myung-bo and captain Son Heung-min, the team is built around fast transitions, organized play, and creative attacking football
Group A.shortDescription: South Korea’s national team enters the 2026 World Cup with speed, discipline, and technical quality, aiming to continue its strong football tradition on the world stage
Group A.title: South Korea

Group A.longDescription: Czechia returns to the FIFA World Cup after 20 years, bringing a team built on organization, intensity, and collective play. Led by coach Miroslav Koubek and featuring players such as Patrik Schick and Tomáš Souček, the squad combines experienced leaders with emerging talent. Known for their physical presence, structured defense, and danger from set pieces, Czechia aims to reestablish itself and compete with Europe’s strongest football nations on the world stage
Group A.shortDescription: Czechia enters the 2026 World Cup with discipline, physical strength, and a balanced squad aiming to make a strong return to football’s biggest stage
Group A.title: Czhechia
